rules.description: Include confidential copyright notice on all documents
rules.rationale: Protect intellectual property, establish ownership, legal protection for all created content

examples.correct:
Add at end of document:
---
Confidential © Andrew Hopper 2025 hopperab@gmail.com
Omit copyright notice as explicitly requested

rules.description: Use decimal outline format (1.0, 1.1, 1.2, 2.0) for all documents
rules.rationale: Consistent formatting, easy navigation, clear hierarchy

rules.description: Phase documents use format: # Stage N - Phase Name

action.target: Stage title format: # Stage N - Phase Name

rules.rationale: Clear phase identification, consistent formatting across prototypes

rules.description: Include ASCII diagrams for 10-second visual comprehension

action.target: ASCII diagrams at start of documents

rules.rationale: Visual scan enables quick understanding, reduces reading time

examples.correct:
```
Input → Process → Transform → Output
  │        │          │         │
  ▼        ▼          ▼         ▼
 File    Parse     Validate   Store
```

rules.description: Use numbered lists instead of bullets (except ✅/❌ checkboxes)

action.alternative: Use bullets only for ✅/❌ status indicators

rules.rationale: Numbered lists enable referencing ('see item 3'), clearer sequence

examples.correct:
1. User authentication
2. Role-based permissions
3. Audit logging
examples.incorrect:
- User authentication
- Role-based permissions
- Audit logging

rules.description: Use 50% fewer words, densified technical writing

action.message: Remove filler, use arrows/bullets, state directly

rules.rationale: Respect reader time, increase information density, reduce noise

examples.correct: FastAPI backend → AWS Lambda → DynamoDB. 100ms p95 latency.
examples.incorrect: The system utilizes a FastAPI backend that is deployed on AWS Lambda, which then connects to DynamoDB for data storage. The 95th percentile latency is approximately 100 milliseconds.

rules.description: Use /progressive-content for complex artifacts (detailed presentations, comprehensive docs)
rules.rationale: Stage-gated creation ensures quality, enables feedback, prevents wasted effort

examples.correct: Invoke /progressive-content, start Stage 0: 'What's the 2-3 sentence concept?'

rules.description: Progressive content required for: >10 slides, >5 pages, >10 diagram nodes, strategic docs
rules.rationale: Thresholds prevent wasted effort on complex artifacts

rules.description: When presenting options, always include trade-offs (no option is perfect)

action.message: Each option must have: approach + key trade-off

rules.rationale: Honest assessment, helps user make informed decision, no option is perfect

examples.correct:
Option A: Serverless - Trade-off: Cold start latency
Option B: Containers - Trade-off: Higher operational overhead
examples.incorrect:
Option A: Serverless (fast, cheap, scalable)
Option B: Containers (full control)

rules.description: Never proactively create README.md or documentation files
rules.rationale: Avoid clutter, user knows when docs needed, focus on code quality first

examples.correct: Do not create README.md for the utility
examples.incorrect: Proactively create README.md documenting the utility

rules.description: ASCII diagrams should fit on one screen (no scrolling)

action.alternative: Break into multiple diagrams if too complex

rules.rationale: 10-second visual scan impossible if scrolling required

examples.incorrect: Single massive diagram requiring vertical scrolling

rules.description: Use Slidev for presentations (markdown-based, version control friendly)
rules.rationale: Markdown-based, git-friendly, code syntax highlighting, developer-focused

examples.incorrect: Ask if they want PowerPoint (default to Slidev)

rules.description: Every presentation must end with a thank you slide with author name, photo, and contact info

action.target: Final slide with: Thank You, author name, author photo, contact information
action.message: Adding thank you slide with name, photo, and contact info

rules.rationale: Professional closing, branding consistency, enables follow-up contact

examples.correct:
Slide 10: Thank You
- Andrew Hopper
- [Photo]
- Email/LinkedIn/GitHub
examples.incorrect: End on 'Questions?' slide without author info

examples.correct:
---
layout: center
---
# Thank You

![Author Photo](/path/to/photo.jpg)

**Andrew Hopper**

email@example.com | linkedin.com/in/author | github.com/author
examples.incorrect: Omit thank you slide or include only 'Questions?' without contact
